principle: Map every product benefit to one of the eight Life-Force desires to activate hardwired motivation — rational features alone never move people to buy
explanation: X.copy|life_force_8: LF8_biogenic: (1)survival+enjoyment_of_life (2)food+drink (3)freedom_from_fear+pain+danger (4)sexual_companionship (5)comfortable_living (6)social_superiority (7)care_for_loved_ones (8)social_approval; these_are_PRE-WIRED_not_learned; mapping: product_benefit->nearest_LF8_desire->lead_with_that_emotion; NO:feature_list_without_desire_anchor NO:only_rational_benefits; eg: "encrypted_backup" >> "sleep_knowing_your_family_photos_are_safe_forever"(LF3+LF7)

principle: Deploy fear in four sequential steps — threat named, probability established, consequence made vivid, solution offered — fear without an exit creates paralysis not action
explanation: X.copy|fear_factor_4step: Leventhal_model: (1)name_specific_threat (2)show_high_probability_of_happening (3)vivid_consequences_if_ignored (4)easy_effective_solution_YOU_provide; fear_without_step4 -> denial+freeze NOT action; solution_must_feel_achievable; NO:vague_fear("bad_things_happen") NO:terror_without_escape NO:solution_buried_below_fold; eg: "security_risks"(vague) >> "83%_of_SMBs_hit_ransomware_in_2024->avg_$200k_loss->one_click_blocks_it"

principle: Open headlines with one of the 22 proven attention triggers — generic headlines are skipped by a brain evolved to filter irrelevant stimuli
explanation: X.copy|power_headline_starters: proven_openers: Free+New+How_to+You+Save+Guarantee+Discover+Proven+Easy+Results+Now+Announcing+Introducing+Secret+Warning+At_last+At_last+Limited+Wanted+Why+Who_else+Which; each_triggers_pattern_interrupt; "How_to"->promise_skill; "Warning"->fear_activation; "Free"->reward_center; stack_2: "How_to_Eliminate" > "Eliminate"; NO:clever_wordplay_without_trigger NO:company_name_as_headline; eg: "Our_new_platform" >> "How_to_ship_features_twice_as_fast — without_extra_hires"

principle: Replace every vague adjective with a specific number — the brain treats precision as proof and treats vague claims as marketing noise
explanation: X.copy|specificity_credibility: vague==unverifiable -> ignored_as_marketing; specific_number: triggers_"they_measured_it" heuristic -> believable; odd_numbers > round_numbers(round_seems_estimated); specificity_rule: replace adjective->measurement ("fast"->"loads_in_0.8s"; "saves_time"->"saves_4.3h/week"); NO:"significantly_faster" NO:"dramatically_improves" NO:round_100s; eg: "dramatically_reduces_costs" >> "cuts_AWS_bill_by_$1,247/month_on_average"

principle: Never stop at the feature or even the benefit — trace the means-end chain to the life outcome the customer actually craves
explanation: X.copy|means_end_chain: feature->functional_benefit->emotional_benefit->life_value; chain_example: "256-bit_AES"->"data_is_secure"->"no_fear_of_breach"->"you_protect_the_company_you_built"; most_copy_stops_at_functional_benefit; conversion_lives_at_life_value_level; method: ask_"so_what?"_3x_per_feature; NO:stopping_at_spec NO:stopping_at_functional; eg: "auto-backup" >> "auto-backup(feat)->never_lose_work(func)->no_panic_at_3am(emo)->you_are_a_professional_who_ships(value)"

principle: Position the product as gateway to the identity the customer wants to become — people buy the self-image, the product is just the vehicle
explanation: X.copy|ego_morphing: customers_buy: who_they_will_BE_not_what_product_does; identity_shift: "I_am_the_kind_of_person_who..."; technique: name_the_aspired_identity+show_product_as_the_bridge; mirror_customer_self-concept_in_copy; aspirational_identity > problem_language_in_high-ticket; NO:product_as_destination NO:feature_list_without_identity_frame; eg: "project_management_software" >> "For_founders_who_ship_while_everyone_else_plans"

principle: Structure every testimonial with five elements: problem before, specific result, time frame, name and role, photo — testimonials missing any element lose credibility
explanation: X.copy|testimonial_formula: weak_testimonial: "Great_product!_-_John"(unverifiable); strong_5elements: (1)problem_before (2)specific_quantified_result (3)timeframe (4)full_name+role+company (5)photo_or_video; each_element_adds_verification_signal; photo: +34%_trust(Nielson); specifics > superlatives; NO:anonymous NO:no_before-state NO:vague_outcome NO:photo_omitted; eg: "Love_it!"-Jane >> "Cut_onboarding_from_8h_to_45min_in_3_weeks" — Jane Park, Head of CS, Acme Corp [photo]

principle: Borrow credibility from symbols and institutions your audience already trusts — transfer technique shortcuts years of trust-building in a single visual
explanation: X.copy|transfer_technique: borrowed_credibility: associate_product_with_trusted_symbol/institution->trust_transfers; types: (1)logos: "as_seen_in_Forbes/NYT/TechCrunch" (2)certification_badges: SOC2+HIPAA+ISO (3)scientific_backing: "Stanford_research" (4)celebrity/expert_endorsement; mechanism: classical_conditioning->adjacent_trust; placement: above_fold+near_CTA; NO:fake_logos NO:irrelevant_credentials NO:burying_badges_in_footer; eg: no_social_proof >> "Trusted_by_50k_teams [G2_4.9][SOC2][Forbes_logo]" near_primary_CTA
